docs.title: ai-config
docs.text: Declarative plugin manager for Claude Code.

docs.title: Why This Exists
docs.text:
Claude Code plugins are useful. They let you extend Claude with custom skills, hooks, and MCP servers. The problem is managing them.

Without ai-config, you're running claude plugin install and claude plugin marketplace add commands by hand across machines. There's no config file. No way to version control your setup. No way to share it.

ai-config fixes that. You write a YAML file describing what plugins you want, and it handles the rest.

Or more simply, run ai-config init and it writes the config for you.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
uv tool install git+https://github.com/safurrier/ai-config

This installs ai-config globally. Run ai-config --help to verify.

docs.title: For Development
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/safurrier/ai-config.git
cd ai-config
just setup    # Install dependencies
just check    # Run lint, type check, tests

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
1. Create your config

ai-config init

Interactive wizard walks you through adding marketplaces and plugins. Creates .ai-config/config.yaml.

2. Sync to install plugins

ai-config sync

Installs/uninstalls plugins to match your config. Run this after editing config.yaml.

3. Check status

ai-config status

Shows what's installed vs what's in config.

docs.title: What's Next
docs.text:
• Commands - Full command reference
  • Configuration - Config file format and examples

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Complete reference for all ai-config commands.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Command What it does init Interactive config generator sync Install/uninstall plugins to match config status Show what's currently installed watch Auto-sync on file changes update Update plugins to latest versions doctor Validate setup and show fix hints plugin create Scaffold a new plugin cache clear Clear the plugin cache

docs.title: Global Options
docs.text:
All commands support these options:

ai-config [OPTIONS] COMMAND

Options:
  -c, --config PATH  Path to config file
  --verbose         Enable verbose output
  --help           Show help message

docs.title: init
docs.text:
Interactive wizard for creating or updating your config file.

ai-config init

Options:

  • --non-interactive - Create config with defaults (no prompts)
  • --force - Overwrite existing config

The wizard walks you through:

  1. Adding marketplaces (GitHub repos with plugins)
  2. Selecting plugins from those marketplaces
  3. Choosing install scope (user or project)

Creates .ai-config/config.yaml in current directory.

docs.title: sync
docs.text:
Make installed plugins match your config file.

ai-config sync

Options:

  • --fresh - Clear cache and reinstall everything
  • --dry-run - Show what would change without doing it

What it does:

  • Installs plugins listed in config but not installed
  • Uninstalls plugins installed but not in config
  • Updates plugin configurations

docs.title: status
docs.text:
Show current state of marketplaces and plugins.

ai-config status

Displays:

  • Configured marketplaces and their status
  • Installed plugins (from config and extra)
  • Any sync issues

docs.title: watch
docs.text:
Auto-sync when config or plugin files change.

ai-config watch

Options:

  • --debounce SECONDS - Wait time before syncing (default: 1.0)

Useful during plugin development. Watches:

  • .ai-config/config.yaml
  • Plugin directories

Press Ctrl+C to stop.

docs.title: update
docs.text:
Update all plugins to their latest versions.

ai-config update

Options:

  • --marketplace NAME - Update only plugins from specific marketplace

docs.title: doctor
docs.text:
Validate your setup and find problems.

ai-config doctor

Options:

  • --verbose - Show detailed validation info
  • --fix - Attempt to fix found issues

Checks:

  • Marketplace URLs are reachable
  • Plugins are properly installed
  • Skills have required fields
  • Hooks are executable
  • MCP server configs are valid

docs.title: plugin create
docs.text:
Scaffold a new plugin.

ai-config plugin create NAME

Options:

  • --marketplace NAME - Create in specific marketplace
  • --type TYPE - Plugin type (skill, hook, mcp)

Creates plugin directory with:

  • plugin.yaml - Plugin metadata
  • Appropriate starter files based on type

docs.title: cache clear
docs.text:
Clear the plugin cache.

ai-config cache clear

Forces fresh downloads on next sync. Use when plugins seem stale.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: ai-config uses a YAML file to declare your plugins and marketplaces.

docs.title: Config File Location
docs.text:
ai-config looks for config in this order:

  1. .ai-config/config.yaml (project-local)
  2. ~/.ai-config/config.yaml (global)

You can also specify a path with -c /path/to/config.yaml.

docs.title: Config Structure
docs.text:
version: 1
targets:
  - type: claude
    config:
      marketplaces:
        # Marketplace definitions
      plugins:
        # Plugin references

docs.title: Targets
docs.text:
Currently only Claude Code is supported. Future versions may add Codex CLI and OpenCode.

targets:
  - type: claude
    config:
      # Claude-specific config

docs.title: Marketplaces
docs.text:
Marketplaces are GitHub repositories containing plugins.

marketplaces:
  claude-code-tutorial:
    source: github
    repo: safurrier/claude-code-tutorial

  my-plugins:
    source: github
    repo: myorg/my-plugins
    branch: main  # optional, defaults to main

Each marketplace has a name (used to reference plugins) and a source config.

docs.title: Plugins
docs.text:
Plugins reference items from marketplaces.

plugins:
  - id: claude-code-tutorial@claude-code-tutorial
    scope: user
    enabled: true

  - id: my-plugin@my-plugins
    scope: project
    enabled: true

Plugin ID format: plugin-name@marketplace-name

Scopes:

  • user - Installed to ~/.claude/plugins/, available everywhere
  • project - Installed to .claude/plugins/, only for current project

docs.title: Full Example
docs.text:
version: 1
targets:
  - type: claude
    config:
      marketplaces:
        claude-code-tutorial:
          source: github
          repo: safurrier/claude-code-tutorial

        company-plugins:
          source: github
          repo: mycompany/claude-plugins

      plugins:
        # Tutorial plugin for learning Claude Code
        - id: claude-code-tutorial@claude-code-tutorial
          scope: user
          enabled: true

        # Company-wide coding standards
        - id: coding-standards@company-plugins
          scope: user
          enabled: true

        # Project-specific tooling (only in this repo)
        - id: project-tools@company-plugins
          scope: project
          enabled: true

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
You can use environment variables in config:

marketplaces:
  private-plugins:
    source: github
    repo: ${GITHUB_ORG}/private-plugins

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
Run ai-config doctor to validate your config:

ai-config doctor --verbose

This checks:

  • YAML syntax is valid
  • Required fields are present
  • Marketplace repos are accessible
  • Plugin references resolve
